findings.message: DeleteButton triggers an irreversible delete with no confirmation step. A misclick permanently removes the item with no recovery path.
findings.suggestion: Show a confirmation dialog (e.g. window.confirm, a modal, or an inline 'Are you sure?' prompt) before issuing the DELETE request.

findings.message: The button has no disabled-while-pending state. Multiple rapid clicks will fire duplicate DELETE requests and the UI gives no indication that work is in progress.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a loading boolean (useState), set it true before the fetch and false in a finally block, and pass disabled={loading} to the button. Optionally change the label to 'Deleting…' while pending.

findings.message: The fetch response status is never checked. A 4xx/5xx from the server is silently ignored and the page still reloads, giving the user no indication that the delete actually failed.
findings.suggestion: Check response.ok (or response.status) after the await and branch into an error state (e.g. set an error message) instead of proceeding to reload on failure.

findings.message: window.location.reload() is called unconditionally even when the network request throws (e.g. offline / CORS error), because the async handleDelete has no try/catch. An unhandled rejection will silently abort the reload, leaving the UI frozen with no feedback.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the fetch and reload in a try/catch block. On catch, surface an error message to the user rather than swallowing the failure.
